seriesTitle: Leviticus: Holiness and the Way to God
subtitle: Sacrifice, Purity, and the Call to Be Holy
description: Leviticus isn't a rulebook — it's a blueprint for how a holy God can live with an unholy people. Every sacrifice points forward to the one that ended all sacrifices, and the call to holiness is not a burden but an invitation.

aboutSource.name: The Bible Project
aboutSource.description: The Bible Project creates free animated videos, podcasts, and educational resources that explore the Bible as a unified story leading to Jesus.

days.title: Leviticus
days.subtitle: Holiness and the Way to God

reflection.prompt: From everything in this video — what moment stayed with you most?

choices.label: Be holy as I am holy
choices.deepQuestion: Holiness in Leviticus isn't about being perfect — it's about being set apart for God's purposes. What is one area of your life where you sense God is asking you to be distinct from the world around you? What makes that hard?

choices.label: The weight of what sin costs
choices.deepQuestion: The sacrificial system made the cost of sin visible and tangible — an animal died in your place. How seriously do you actually reckon with what Christ absorbed on your behalf? How does that land today?

choices.label: Love your neighbor as yourself
choices.deepQuestion: In the middle of all the purity codes, God commands love for the neighbor. Who is the neighbor right in front of you that you've been overlooking, avoiding, or tolerating rather than actually loving?

days.abide: God, you are holy — and you call us to be holy not because we can earn your presence, but because you have already given it. Every sacrifice in Leviticus was a shadow of the one that ended all sacrifices. Jesus absorbed what we owed. We want to live like that cost something. Make us people who take your holiness seriously and your grace personally.
